revision.comment: 高祖山のページを新規作成
revision.text:
[[File:高祖山.jpg|500px|right|thumb|高祖山]]
[[福岡県]][[福岡市]]と[[糸島市]]の境に位置する標高416mの山。 西斜面一帯に[[天平勝宝]]8年6月（[[756年]]）から[[神護景雲]]2年2月（[[768年]]）まで約12年の歳月をかけて、[[吉備真備]]（途中から[[佐伯今毛人]]に交代）が築いた[[怡土城]]があった。 廃城になった時期は不明だが、この遺構を利用し、[[建長]]元年（[[1249年]]）、[[原田種継]]、[[原田種頼]]父子が[[高祖城]]を築城した。 山頂に上ノ城（本丸）と下ノ城（二ノ丸）がある。

[[File:高祖山.jpg|500px|right|thumb|高祖山]]
[[福岡県]][[福岡市]]と[[糸島市]]の境に位置する標高416mの山。 西斜面一帯に[[天平勝宝]]8年6月（[[756年]]）から[[神護景雲]]2年2月（[[768年]]）まで約12年の歳月をかけて、[[吉備真備]]（途中から[[佐伯今毛人]]に交代）が築いた[[怡土城]]があった。 廃城になった時期は不明だが、この遺構を利用し、[[建長]]元年（[[1249年]]）、[[原田種継]]、[[原田種頼]]父子が[[高祖城]]を築城した。 山頂に上ノ城（本丸）と下ノ城（二ノ丸）がある。
[[Category:福岡県の市町村|たかすやま]] [[Category:糸島市|たかすやま]]

[[File:高祖山.jpg|500px|right|thumb|高祖山]]
[[福岡県]][[福岡市]]と[[糸島市]]の境に位置する標高416mの山。 西斜面一帯に[[天平勝宝]]8年6月（[[756年]]）から[[神護景雲]]2年2月（[[768年]]）まで約12年の歳月をかけて、[[吉備真備]]（途中から[[佐伯今毛人]]に交代）が築いた[[怡土城]]があった。 廃城になった時期は不明だが、この遺構を利用し、[[建長]]元年（[[1249年]]）、[[原田種継]]、[[原田種頼]]父子が[[高祖城]]を築城した。 山頂に上ノ城（本丸）と下ノ城（二ノ丸）がある。
[[Category:福岡県の山|たかすやま]] [[Category:糸島市|たかすやま]]

revision.comment: 項目を分け文章を追加しました。
revision.text:
{{複数の問題 |出典の明記=2014年9月11日 (木) 15:59 (UTC) |特筆性=2014年9月11日 (木) 15:59 (UTC) }} {{Infobox 山 |名称 = 高祖山 |画像 = [[File:高祖山.jpg|300px|高祖山]] |画像キャプション = 高祖山遠景 |標高 = 416 |座標 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|32|54|N|130|16|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} |所在地 = {{JPN}}
[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]・[[福岡市]][[西区 (福岡市)|西区]] |山系= |種類 = |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Embedmap|130.268646|33.548313|300}}高祖山の位置{{日本の位置情報|33|32|54|130|16|07|高祖山|33.548313,130.268646|高祖山}} }} '''高祖山'''（たかすやま）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]]と[[糸島市]]の境に位置する標高416mの山である。高祖山には古代の[[怡土城]]と中世の[[高祖城]]（原田城）の跡がある。 == 名称 == 牛が長々と寝そべっている姿から臥牛山とも言われ、[[糸島]]のかなりの地域からその山容を望むことができる。高祖山には南にもう一つの峰があり、これを[[くしふる山]]という。 == 怡土城 == {{main|怡土城}} 西斜面一帯には、[[天平勝宝]]8年6月（[[756年]]）から[[神護景雲]]2年2月（[[768年]]）まで約12年の歳月をかけて、[[吉備真備]]（途中から[[佐伯今毛人]]に交代）が築いた[[怡土城]]があった。 == 高祖城 == {{main|高祖城}} [[怡土城]]が廃城になった時期は不明だが、この遺構を利用して、[[建長]]元年（[[1249年]]）、[[原田種継]]・[[原田種頼|種頼]]父子が[[高祖城]]を築城した。山頂には上ノ城（本丸）と下ノ城（二ノ丸）がある。山腹には[[高祖城]]主[[原田氏]]の崇敬が厚かった[[高祖神社]]がある。 == 登山 == 今宿野外活動センターを拠点に、南の[[高地山]]、東の[[叶岳]]とミニ縦走を楽しむことができる。その他高来寺や如意、[[高祖神社]]裏手からの登山道がある。遊歩道はよく整備されており、1〜2時間ほどで山頂に到達できる。山頂は木が茂り眺望があまり良くないが、途中の尾根から東に[[博多湾]]や[[福岡市]]街、西に[[可也山]]や[[浮岳]]、[[羽金山]]、[[雷山]]など[[背振山脈]]系の山々が見られる。 == 参考文献 == * 『糸島郡史』福岡県郷土史叢刊（[[1927年]]） * 『改正原田記』[[児玉琢]]/著（[[1817年]]） == 外部リンク == * [http://yokanavi.com/jp/landmark/index/124 高祖山 - 福岡・博多の観光案内サイト よかなび] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 高祖山登山歩道 - 福岡県観光情報 クロスロードふくおか] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html 怡土城跡（いとじょうあと） - 糸島市ホームページ] {{デフォルトソート:たかすやま}} [[Category:福岡県の山]] [[Category:福岡市西区の地理]] [[Category:糸島市]]

{{複数の問題 |出典の明記=2014年9月11日 (木) 15:59 (UTC) |特筆性=2014年9月11日 (木) 15:59 (UTC) }} {{Infobox 山 |名称 = 高祖山 |画像 = [[File:高祖山.jpg|300px|高祖山]] |画像キャプション = 高祖山遠景 |標高 = 416 |座標 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|32|54|N|130|16|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} |所在地 = {{JPN}}

{{Infobox 山 |名称 = 高祖山 |画像 = [[File:高祖山.jpg|300px|高祖山]] |画像キャプション = 高祖山遠景 |標高 = 416 |座標 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|32|54|N|130|16|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} |所在地 = {{JPN}}

[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]・[[福岡市]][[西区 (福岡市)|西区]] |山系= |種類 = |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Embedmap|130.268646|33.548313|300}}高祖山の位置{{日本の位置情報|33|32|54|130|16|07|高祖山|33.548313,130.268646|高祖山}} }} '''高祖山'''（たかすやま）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]]と[[糸島市]]の境に位置する標高416mの山である。高祖山には古代の[[怡土城]]と中世の[[高祖城]]（原田城）の跡がある。 == 名称 == 「高祖山」のその名の由来は正確には不明で、古い文献には「高祖」「高祖の峰」「高祖山」とある。西の麓に[[高祖神社]]が存在する事から、この神社が山の名の由来、あるいは、先に山の「高祖」から「高祖神社」となったのか、はっきりとは決め難いが、「高祖」の名称はこの一帯にあったらしい。
また、その山容が、牛が長々と寝そべっている姿のように見える（特に[[志登神社]]から見るその山容は牛が寝そべっている姿に見える事）から'''臥牛山'''と地元では言われ、[[糸島市]]内のかなりの地域からその山容を望むことができる。
高祖山には南にもう一つの峰があり、これを「'''くしふる山'''」と呼んでいた、と推定される（文献には、「椚(クヌギ)～、民家の後ろにあるをクシフル山と云ひしを誤りて椚と云ふに至れり」とある）『糸島郡史』福岡県郷土史叢刊（[[1927年]]）。 == 怡土城 == {{main|怡土城}} 西斜面一帯には、[[天平勝宝]]8年6月（[[756年]]）から[[神護景雲]]2年2月（[[768年]]）まで約12年の歳月をかけて、[[吉備真備]]（途中から[[佐伯今毛人]]に交代）が築いた[[怡土城]]があった。 == 高祖城 == {{main|高祖城}} [[怡土城]]が廃城になった時期は不明だが、この遺構を利用して、[[建長]]元年（[[1249年]]）、[[原田種継]]・[[原田種頼|種頼]]父子が[[高祖城]]を築城した。山頂には上ノ城（本丸）と下ノ城（二ノ丸）がある。山腹には[[高祖城]]主[[原田氏]]の崇敬が厚かった[[高祖神社]]と原田家菩提寺の[[金龍寺]]がある『改正原田記』[[児玉琢]]/著（[[1817年]]）。 == 登山 == 今宿野外活動センターを拠点に、南の[[高地山]]、東の[[叶岳]]とミニ縦走を楽しむことができる。その他高来寺や如意、[[高祖神社]]裏手からの登山道がある。遊歩道はよく整備されており、1〜2時間ほどで山頂に到達できる。山頂は木が茂り眺望があまり良くないが、途中の尾根から東に[[博多湾]]や[[福岡市]]街、西に[[可也山]]や[[浮嶽]]、[[羽金山]]、[[雷山]]など[[脊振山]]系の山々が見られる。 == 参考文献 == 　 == 外部リンク == * [http://yokanavi.com/jp/landmark/index/124 高祖山 - 福岡・博多の観光案内サイト よかなび] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 高祖山登山歩道 - 福岡県観光情報 クロスロードふくおか] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html 怡土城跡（いとじょうあと） - 糸島市ホームページ] {{デフォルトソート:たかすやま}} [[Category:福岡県の山]] [[Category:福岡市西区の地理]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]]

[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]・[[福岡市]][[西区 (福岡市)|西区]] |山系= |種類 = |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Embedmap|130.268646|33.548313|300}}高祖山の位置{{日本の位置情報|33|32|54|130|16|07|高祖山|33.548313,130.268646|高祖山}} }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), sanchou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|山頂}}]] '''高祖山'''（たかすやま）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]]と[[糸島市]]の境に位置する標高416mの山である。古代の[[怡土城]]跡と中世の[[高祖城]]跡（原田城跡）がある。 == 名称 == 「高祖山」のその名の由来は正確には不明で、古い文献には「高祖」「高祖の峰」「高祖山」とある。西の麓に[[高祖神社]]が存在する事から、この神社が山の名の由来、あるいは、先に山の「高祖」から「高祖神社」となったのか、はっきりとは決め難いが、「高祖」の名称はこの一帯にあったらしい。

高祖山には南にもう一つの峰があり、これを「'''くしふる山'''」と呼んでいた、と推定される（文献には、「椚(クヌギ)～、民家の後ろにあるをクシフル山と云ひしを誤りて椚と云ふに至れり」とある）『糸島郡誌』福岡県郷土史叢刊（[[1927年]]）。 == 怡土城 == {{main|怡土城}} 西斜面一帯には、[[天平勝宝]]8年6月（[[756年]]）から[[神護景雲]]2年2月（[[768年]]）まで約12年の歳月をかけて、[[吉備真備]]（途中から[[佐伯今毛人]]に交代）が築いた[[怡土城]]があった。 == 高祖城 == {{main|高祖城}} [[怡土城]]が廃城になった時期は不明だが、この遺構を利用して、[[建長]]元年（[[1249年]]）、[[原田種継]]・[[原田種頼|種頼]]父子が[[高祖城]]を築城した。山頂には上ノ城（本丸）と下ノ城（二ノ丸）がある。山腹には[[高祖城]]主[[原田氏]]の崇敬が厚かった[[高祖神社]]と原田家菩提寺の[[金龍寺]]がある『改正原田記』[[児玉琢]]/著（[[1817年]]）。 == 登山 == 今宿野外活動センターを拠点に、南の[[高地山]]、東の[[叶岳]]とミニ縦走を楽しむことができる。その他高来寺や如意、[[高祖神社]]裏手からの登山道がある。遊歩道はよく整備されており、1〜2時間ほどで山頂に到達できる。山頂は木が茂り眺望があまり良くないが、途中の尾根から東に[[博多湾]]や[[福岡市]]街、西に[[可也山]]や[[浮嶽]]、[[羽金山]]、[[雷山]]など[[脊振山]]系の山々が見られる。 == 出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ 高祖山 - 福岡市の公式シティガイド よかなび] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 高祖山登山歩道 - 福岡県観光情報 クロスロードふくおか] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html 怡土城跡（いとじょうあと） - 糸島市ホームページ] {{デフォルトソート:たかすやま}} [[Category:福岡県の山]] [[Category:福岡市の地理]] [[Category:西区 (福岡市)]] [[Category:糸島市の地理]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]]

[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]・[[福岡市]][[西区 (福岡市)|西区]] |山系= |種類 = |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Embedmap|130.268646|33.548313|300}}高祖山の位置{{日本の位置情報|33|32|54|130|16|07|高祖山|33.548313,130.268646|高祖山}} }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), sanchou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|山頂}}]] '''高祖山'''（たかすやま）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]]と[[糸島市]]の境に位置する標高416mの山である。古代の[[怡土城]]跡と中世の[[高祖城]]跡（原田城跡）がある。 == 名称 == 「高祖山」のその名の由来は正確には不明で、古い文献には「高祖」「高祖の峰」「高祖山」とある。西の麓に[[高祖神社]]が存在する事から、この神社が山の名の由来、あるいは、先に山の「高祖」から「高祖神社」となったのか、はっきりとは決め難いが、「高祖」の名称はこの一帯にあったらしい。
また、その山容が、牛が長々と寝そべっている姿のように見える（特に[[志登神社]]から見るその山容は牛が寝そべっている姿に見える事）から'''臥牛山'''と地元では言われ、[[糸島市]]内のかなりの地域からその山容を望むことができる。
高祖山には南にもう一つの峰があり、これを「'''くしふる山'''」と呼んでいた、と推定される（文献には、「椚(クヌギ)～、民家の後ろにあるをクシフル山と云ひしを誤りて椚と云ふに至れり」とある）『糸島郡誌』福岡県郷土史叢刊（[[1927年]]）。 == 怡土城 == {{main|怡土城}} 西斜面一帯には、[[天平勝宝]]8年6月（[[756年]]）から[[神護景雲]]2年2月（[[768年]]）まで約12年の歳月をかけて、[[吉備真備]]（途中から[[佐伯今毛人]]に交代）が築いた[[怡土城]]があった。 == 高祖城 == {{main|高祖城}} [[怡土城]]が廃城になった時期は不明だが、この遺構を利用して、[[建長]]元年（[[1249年]]）、[[原田種継]]・[[原田種頼|種頼]]父子が[[高祖城]]を築城した。山頂には上ノ城（本丸）と下ノ城（二ノ丸）がある。山腹には[[高祖城]]主[[原田氏]]の崇敬が厚かった[[高祖神社]]と原田家菩提寺の[[金龍寺]]がある『改正原田記』[[児玉琢]]/著（[[1817年]]）。 == 登山 == 今宿野外活動センターを拠点に、南の高地山、東の[[叶岳]]とミニ縦走を楽しむことができる。その他高来寺や如意、[[高祖神社]]裏手からの登山道がある。遊歩道はよく整備されており、1〜2時間ほどで山頂に到達できる。山頂は木が茂り眺望があまり良くないが、途中の尾根から東に[[博多湾]]や[[福岡市]]街、西に[[可也山]]や[[浮嶽]]、[[羽金山]]、[[雷山]]など[[脊振山]]系の山々が見られる。 == 出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ 高祖山 - 福岡市の公式シティガイド よかなび] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 高祖山登山歩道 - 福岡県観光情報 クロスロードふくおか] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html 怡土城跡（いとじょうあと） - 糸島市ホームページ] {{デフォルトソート:たかすやま}} [[Category:アジアの山]] [[Category:福岡県の山]] [[Category:福岡市の地理]] [[Category:西区 (福岡市)]] [[Category:糸島市の地理]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]]

高祖山には南にもう一つの峰があり、これを「'''くしふる山'''」と呼んでいた、と推定される（文献には、「椚(クヌギ)～、民家の後ろにあるをクシフル山と云ひしを誤りて椚と云ふに至れり」とある）『糸島郡誌』福岡県郷土史叢刊（[[1927年]]）。 == 怡土城 == {{main|怡土城}} 西斜面一帯には、[[天平勝宝]]8年6月（[[756年]]）から[[神護景雲]]2年2月（[[768年]]）まで約12年の歳月をかけて、[[吉備真備]]（途中から[[佐伯今毛人]]に交代）が築いた[[怡土城]]があった。 == 高祖城 == {{main|高祖城}} [[怡土城]]が廃城になった時期は不明だが、この遺構を利用して、[[建長]]元年（[[1249年]]）、[[原田種継]]・[[原田種頼|種頼]]父子が[[高祖城]]を築城した。山頂には上ノ城（本丸）と下ノ城（二ノ丸）がある。山腹には[[高祖城]]主[[原田氏]]の崇敬が厚かった[[高祖神社]]と原田家菩提寺の[[金龍寺]]がある『改正原田記』[[児玉琢]]/著（[[1817年]]）。 == 登山 == 今宿野外活動センターを拠点に、南の高地山、東の[[叶岳]]とミニ縦走を楽しむことができる。その他高来寺や如意、[[高祖神社]]裏手からの登山道がある。遊歩道はよく整備されており、1〜2時間ほどで山頂に到達できる。山頂は木が茂り眺望があまり良くないが、途中の尾根から東に[[博多湾]]や[[福岡市]]街、西に[[可也山]]や[[浮嶽]]、[[羽金山]]、[[雷山]]など[[脊振山]]系の山々が見られる。 == 出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ 高祖山 - 福岡市の公式シティガイド よかなび] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 高祖山登山歩道 - 福岡県観光情報 クロスロードふくおか] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html 怡土城跡（いとじょうあと） - 糸島市ホームページ] {{デフォルトソート:たかすやま}} [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:福岡県の山]] [[Category:福岡市西区の地理]] [[Category:糸島市の地理]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Takasu | photo = [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), enkei.jpg|300px|Mount Takasu]] | photo_caption = Distant view of Mount Takasu | elevation_m = 416 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|54|N|130|16|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
[[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Itoshima City]] and [[Fukuoka City]] ([[Nishi Ward]]) | mountain_range = | type = | first_ascent = | map = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan|coordinates={{Coord|33.548313|130.268646}}|caption=|width=300|float=center|relief=1}}Location of Mount Takasu{{日本の位置情報|33|32|54|130|16|07|Mount Takasu|33.548313,130.268646|Mount Takasu|nocoord=yes}} | osm_zoom = 10 }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), sanchou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Summit of Mount Takasu}}]] '''Mount Takasu''' (高祖山, Takasu-yama) is a mountain with an elevation of 416 meters located on the border of [[Fukuoka City]] and [[Itoshima City]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The mountain is home to the remains of the ancient {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城}} and the medieval {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城}} (also known as Harada Castle). == Name == The origin of the name "Mount Takasu" is unclear. Historical records refer to it as "Takasu," "Peak of Takasu," and "Mount Takasu." Given the presence of {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}} at the western foot of the mountain, it is uncertain whether the shrine derived its name from the mountain or vice versa. The name "Takasu" appears to have been widely recognized in the region.
The mountain is also locally known as "Sleeping Ox Mountain" (臥牛山, Gagyūzan) due to its resemblance to a reclining ox, particularly when viewed from {{ill|Shito Shrine|ja|志登神社}}. This characteristic is visible from many parts of [[Itoshima City]].
South of Mount Takasu lies another peak historically referred to as "Kushifuru Mountain," according to records that note, "It was mistakenly called Kushifuru Mountain due to its location behind a house."‘Itoshima Gunshi’ Fukuoka Prefecture Historical Collections (1927). == Ito Castle == {{main|Ito Castle}} The western slopes of the mountain hosted {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城}}, constructed by {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} (later replaced by {{ill|Saeki no Imami|ja|佐伯今毛人}}) over 12 years, from June of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 8 (756 CE) to February of {{ill|Jingō-keiun|ja|神護景雲}} 2 (768 CE). == Takasu Castle == {{main|Takasu Castle}} While the exact date of Ito Castle's abandonment is unknown, its ruins were later repurposed. In {{ill|Kenchō|ja|建長}} 1 (1249 CE), {{ill|Harada Tanetsugu|ja|原田種継}} and his son {{ill|Harada Taneyori|ja|原田種頼}} built {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城}} on this site. The summit features two structures: the upper castle (honmaru) and the lower castle (ninomaru). The slopes house {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}}, revered by the Harada clan, and {{ill|Kinryūji Temple|ja|金龍寺 (福岡市)}}, the family temple of the Harada.‘Kaisei Harada-ki’ by Kodama Takku (1817). == Hiking == Mount Takasu offers several hiking routes. The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of [[Hakata Bay]], [[Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|ja|雷山}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Yokanavi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{デフォルトソート:たかすやま}} [[Category:Mountain-related articles]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima City]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[ja:高祖山]]

The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of [[Hakata Bay]], [[Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|ja|雷山}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Yokanavi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{デフォルトソート:たかすやま}} [[Category:Mountain-related articles]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima City]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[category:most recent 500]][[ja:高祖山]]

{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Takasu | photo = [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), enkei.jpg|300px|Mount Takasu]] | photo_caption = Distant view of Mount Takasu | elevation_m = 416 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|54|N|130|16|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
[[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Itoshima City]] and [[Fukuoka City]] ([[Nishi Ward]]) | mountain_range = | type = | first_ascent = | map = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan|coordinates={{Coord|33.548313|130.268646}}|caption=|width=300|float=center|relief=1}}Location of Mount Takasu{{日本の位置情報|33|32|54|130|16|07|Mount Takasu|33.548313,130.268646|Mount Takasu|nocoord=yes}} | osm_zoom = 10 }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), sanchou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Summit of Mount Takasu}}]] '''Mount Takasu''' (高祖山, Takasu-yama) is a mountain with an elevation of 416 meters located on the border of [[Fukuoka City]] and [[Itoshima City]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The mountain is home to the remains of the ancient {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城}} and the medieval {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城}} (also known as Harada Castle). == Name == The origin of the name "Mount Takasu" is unclear. Historical records refer to it as "Takasu," "Peak of Takasu," and "Mount Takasu." Given the presence of {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}} at the western foot of the mountain, it is uncertain whether the shrine derived its name from the mountain or vice versa. The name "Takasu" appears to have been widely recognized in the region.
The mountain is also locally known as "Sleeping Ox Mountain" (臥牛山, Gagyūzan) due to its resemblance to a reclining ox, particularly when viewed from {{ill|Shito Shrine|ja|志登神社}}. This characteristic is visible from many parts of [[Itoshima City]].
South of Mount Takasu lies another peak historically referred to as "Kushifuru Mountain," according to records that note, "It was mistakenly called Kushifuru Mountain due to its location behind a house."‘Itoshima Gunshi’ Fukuoka Prefecture Historical Collections (1927). == Ito Castle == {{main|Ito Castle}} The western slopes of the mountain hosted {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城}}, constructed by {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} (later replaced by {{ill|Saeki no Imami|ja|佐伯今毛人}}) over 12 years, from June of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 8 (756 CE) to February of {{ill|Jingō-keiun|ja|神護景雲}} 2 (768 CE). == Takasu Castle == {{main|Takasu Castle}} While the exact date of Ito Castle's abandonment is unknown, its ruins were later repurposed. In {{ill|Kenchō|ja|建長}} 1 (1249 CE), {{ill|Harada Tanetsugu|ja|原田種継}} and his son {{ill|Harada Taneyori|ja|原田種頼}} built {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城}} on this site. The summit features two structures: the upper castle (honmaru) and the lower castle (ninomaru). The slopes house {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}}, revered by the Harada clan, and {{ill|Kinryūji Temple|ja|金龍寺 (福岡市)}}, the family temple of the Harada.‘Kaisei Harada-ki’ by Kodama Takku (1817). == Hiking == Mount Takasu offers several hiking routes. The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of [[Hakata Bay]], [[Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|ja|雷山}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Yokanavi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{デフォルトソート:たかすやま}} [[Category:Mountain-related articles]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima City]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:高祖山]]

Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of [[Hakata Bay]], [[Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|ja|雷山}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Yokanavi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{デフォルトソート:たかすやま}} [[Category:Mountain-related articles]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima City]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:高祖山]]

{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Takasu | photo = [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), enkei.jpg|300px|Mount Takasu]] | photo_caption = Distant view of Mount Takasu | elevation_m = 416 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|54|N|130|16|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
[[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Itoshima City]] and [[Fukuoka City]] ([[Nishi Ward]]) | mountain_range = | type = | first_ascent = | map = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan|coordinates={{Coord|33.548313|130.268646}}|caption=|width=300|float=center|relief=1}}Location of Mount Takasu{{日本の位置情報|33|32|54|130|16|07|Mount Takasu|33.548313,130.268646|Mount Takasu|nocoord=yes}} | osm_zoom = 10 }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), sanchou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Summit of Mount Takasu}}]] '''Mount Takasu''' (高祖山, Takasu-yama) is a mountain with an elevation of 416 meters located on the border of [[Fukuoka City]] and [[Itoshima City]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The mountain is home to the remains of the ancient {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城}} and the medieval {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城}} (also known as Harada Castle). == Name == The origin of the name "Mount Takasu" is unclear. Historical records refer to it as "Takasu," "Peak of Takasu," and "Mount Takasu." Given the presence of {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}} at the western foot of the mountain, it is uncertain whether the shrine derived its name from the mountain or vice versa. The name "Takasu" appears to have been widely recognized in the region.
The mountain is also locally known as "Sleeping Ox Mountain" (臥牛山, Gagyūzan) due to its resemblance to a reclining ox, particularly when viewed from {{ill|Shito Shrine|ja|志登神社}}. This characteristic is visible from many parts of [[Itoshima City]].
South of Mount Takasu lies another peak historically referred to as "Kushifuru Mountain," according to records that note, "It was mistakenly called Kushifuru Mountain due to its location behind a house."‘Itoshima Gunshi’ Fukuoka Prefecture Historical Collections (1927). == Ito Castle == {{main|Ito Castle}} The western slopes of the mountain hosted {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城}}, constructed by {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} (later replaced by {{ill|Saeki no Imami|ja|佐伯今毛人}}) over 12 years, from June of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 8 (756 CE) to February of {{ill|Jingō-keiun|ja|神護景雲}} 2 (768 CE). == Takasu Castle == {{main|Takasu Castle}} While the exact date of Ito Castle's abandonment is unknown, its ruins were later repurposed. In {{ill|Kenchō|ja|建長}} 1 (1249 CE), {{ill|Harada Tanetsugu|ja|原田種継}} and his son {{ill|Harada Taneyori|ja|原田種頼}} built {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城}} on this site. The summit features two structures: the upper castle (honmaru) and the lower castle (ninomaru). The slopes house {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}}, revered by the Harada clan, and {{ill|Kinryūji Temple|ja|金龍寺 (福岡市)}}, the family temple of the Harada.‘Kaisei Harada-ki’ by Kodama Takku (1817). == Hiking == Mount Takasu offers several hiking routes. The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of [[Hakata Bay]], [[Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|ja|雷山}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Yokanavi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{デフォルトソート:たかすやま}} [[Category:Mountain-related articles]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima City]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:高祖山]]

Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of [[Hakata Bay]], [[Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|ja|雷山}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Yokanavi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{デフォルトソート:たかすやま}} [[Category:Mountain-related articles]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima City]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:Https://lld.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:New pages]] [[lld:Mont Takasu]] [[ja:高祖山]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]]

{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Takasu | photo = [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), enkei.jpg|300px|Mount Takasu]] | photo_caption = Distant view of Mount Takasu | elevation_m = 416 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|54|N|130|16|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
[[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Itoshima City]] and [[Fukuoka City]] ([[Nishi Ward]]) | mountain_range = | type = | first_ascent = | map = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan|coordinates={{Coord|33.548313|130.268646}}|caption=|width=300|float=center|relief=1}}Location of Mount Takasu{{日本の位置情報|33|32|54|130|16|07|Mount Takasu|33.548313,130.268646|Mount Takasu|nocoord=yes}} | osm_zoom = 10 }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), sanchou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Summit of Mount Takasu}}]] '''Mount Takasu''' (高祖山, Takasu-yama) is a mountain with an elevation of 416 meters located on the border of [[Fukuoka City]] and [[Itoshima City]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The mountain is home to the remains of the ancient {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城}} and the medieval {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城}} (also known as Harada Castle). == Name == The origin of the name "Mount Takasu" is unclear. Historical records refer to it as "Takasu," "Peak of Takasu," and "Mount Takasu." Given the presence of {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}} at the western foot of the mountain, it is uncertain whether the shrine derived its name from the mountain or vice versa. The name "Takasu" appears to have been widely recognized in the region.
The mountain is also locally known as "Sleeping Ox Mountain" (臥牛山, Gagyūzan) due to its resemblance to a reclining ox, particularly when viewed from {{ill|Shito Shrine|ja|志登神社}}. This characteristic is visible from many parts of [[Itoshima City]].
South of Mount Takasu lies another peak historically referred to as "Kushifuru Mountain," according to records that note, "It was mistakenly called Kushifuru Mountain due to its location behind a house."‘Itoshima Gunshi’ Fukuoka Prefecture Historical Collections (1927). == Ito Castle == {{main|Ito Castle}} The western slopes of the mountain hosted {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城}}, constructed by {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} (later replaced by {{ill|Saeki no Imami|ja|佐伯今毛人}}) over 12 years, from June of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 8 (756 CE) to February of {{ill|Jingō-keiun|ja|神護景雲}} 2 (768 CE). == Takasu Castle == {{main|Takasu Castle}} While the exact date of Ito Castle's abandonment is unknown, its ruins were later repurposed. In {{ill|Kenchō|ja|建長}} 1 (1249 CE), {{ill|Harada Tanetsugu|ja|原田種継}} and his son {{ill|Harada Taneyori|ja|原田種頼}} built {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城}} on this site. The summit features two structures: the upper castle (honmaru) and the lower castle (ninomaru). The slopes house {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}}, revered by the Harada clan, and {{ill|Kinryūji Temple|ja|金龍寺 (福岡市)}}, the family temple of the Harada.‘Kaisei Harada-ki’ by Kodama Takku (1817). == Hiking == Mount Takasu offers several hiking routes. The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of [[Hakata Bay]], [[Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|ja|雷山}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Yokanavi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{デフォルトソート:たかすやま}} [[Category:Mountain-related articles]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima City]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:Https://lld.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:New pages]] [[lld:Mont Takasu]] [[ja:高祖山]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]]

The slopes house {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}}, revered by the Harada clan, and {{ill|Kinryūji Temple|ja|金龍寺 (福岡市)}}, the family temple of the Harada.‘Kaisei Harada-ki’ by Kodama Takku (1817). == Hiking == Mount Takasu offers several hiking routes. The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of [[Hakata Bay]], [[Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|ja|雷山}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Yokanavi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{citation needed|たかすやま}} [[Category:Mountain-related articles]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima City]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:Https://lld.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:New pages]] [[lld:Mont Takasu]] [[ja:高祖山]] [[Category:pages of u]][[Category:ality]] [[Category:pages of u]][[Category:ality]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]]

{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Takasu | photo = [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), enkei.jpg|300px|Mount Takasu]] | photo_caption = Distant view of Mount Takasu | elevation_m = 416 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|54|N|130|16|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
[[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Itoshima City]] and [[Fukuoka City]] ([[Nishi Ward]]) | mountain_range = | type = | first_ascent = | map = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan|coordinates={{Coord|33.548313|130.268646}}|caption=|width=300|float=center|relief=1}}Location of Mount Takasu{{日本の位置情報|33|32|54|130|16|07|Mount Takasu|33.548313,130.268646|Mount Takasu|nocoord=yes}} | osm_zoom = 10 }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), sanchou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Summit of Mount Takasu}}]] '''Mount Takasu''' (高祖山, Takasu-yama) is a mountain with an elevation of 416 meters located on the border of [[Fukuoka City]] and [[Itoshima City]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The mountain is home to the remains of the ancient {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城}} and the medieval {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城}} (also known as Harada Castle). == Name == The origin of the name "Mount Takasu" is unclear. Historical records refer to it as "Takasu," "Peak of Takasu," and "Mount Takasu." Given the presence of {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}} at the western foot of the mountain, it is uncertain whether the shrine derived its name from the mountain or vice versa. The name "Takasu" appears to have been widely recognized in the region.
The mountain is also locally known as "Sleeping Ox Mountain" (臥牛山, Gagyūzan) due to its resemblance to a reclining ox, particularly when viewed from {{ill|Shito Shrine|ja|志登神社}}. This characteristic is visible from many parts of [[Itoshima City]].
South of Mount Takasu lies another peak historically referred to as "Kushifuru Mountain," according to records that note, "It was mistakenly called Kushifuru Mountain due to its location behind a house."‘Itoshima Gunshi’ Fukuoka Prefecture Historical Collections (1927). == Ito Castle == {{main|Ito Castle}} The western slopes of the mountain hosted {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城}}, constructed by {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} (later replaced by {{ill|Saeki no Imami|ja|佐伯今毛人}}) over 12 years, from June of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 8 (756 CE) to February of {{ill|Jingō-keiun|ja|神護景雲}} 2 (768 CE). == Takasu Castle == {{main|Takasu Castle}} While the exact date of Ito Castle's abandonment is unknown, its ruins were later repurposed. In {{ill|Kenchō|ja|建長}} 1 (1249 CE), {{ill|Harada Tanetsugu|ja|原田種継}} and his son {{ill|Harada Taneyori|ja|原田種頼}} built {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城}} on this site. The summit features two structures: the upper castle (honmaru) and the lower castle (ninomaru). The slopes house {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}}, revered by the Harada clan, and {{ill|Kinryūji Temple|ja|金龍寺 (福岡市)}}, the family temple of the Harada.‘Kaisei Harada-ki’ by Kodama Takku (1817). == Hiking == Mount Takasu offers several hiking routes. The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of [[Hakata Bay]], [[Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|ja|雷山}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Yokanavi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{citation needed|たかすやま}} [[Category:Mountain-related articles]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima City]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:Https://lld.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:New pages]] [[lld:Mont Takasu]] [[ja:高祖山]] [[Category:pages of u]][[Category:ality]] [[Category:pages of u]][[Category:ality]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mount Takasu]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 3]]

South of Mount Takasu lies another peak historically referred to as "Kushifuru Mountain," according to records that note, "It was mistakenly called Kushifuru Mountain due to its location behind a house."‘Itoshima Gunshi’ Fukuoka Prefecture Historical Collections (1927). == Ito Castle == {{main|Ito Castle}} The western slopes of the mountain hosted {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城}}, constructed by {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} (later replaced by {{ill|Saeki no Imami|ja|佐伯今毛人}}) over 12 years, from June of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 8 (756 CE) to February of {{ill|Jingō-keiun|ja|神護景雲}} 2 (768 CE). == Takasu Castle == {{main|Takasu Castle}} While the exact date of Ito Castle's abandonment is unknown, its ruins were later repurposed. In {{ill|Kenchō|ja|建長}} 1 (1249 CE), {{ill|Harada Tanetsugu|ja|原田種継}} and his son {{ill|Harada Taneyori|ja|原田種頼}} built {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城}} on this site. The summit features two structures: the upper castle (honmaru) and the lower castle (ninomaru). The slopes house {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}}, revered by the Harada clan, and {{ill|Kinryūji Temple|ja|金龍寺 (福岡市)}}, the family temple of the Harada.‘Kaisei Harada-ki’ by Kodama Takku (1817). == Hiking == Mount Takasu offers several hiking routes. The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of [[Hakata Bay]], [[Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|ja|雷山}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Cave Tombvi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{citation needed|たかすやま}} [[Category:Mountain-related articles]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima City]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:New pages]] [[lld:Mont Takasu]] [[ja:高祖山]] [[Category:pages of u]][[Category:ality]] [[Category:pages of u]][[Category:ality]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mount Takasu]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mount Takasu]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mount Takasu}}[[Category:qq]]

Given the presence of {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}} at the western foot of the mountain, it is uncertain whether the shrine derived its name from the mountain or vice versa. The name "Takasu" appears to have been widely recognized in the region.
The mountain is also locally known as "Sleeping Ox Mountain" (臥牛山, Gagyūzan) due to its resemblance to a reclining ox, particularly when viewed from {{ill|Shito Shrine|ja|志登神社}}. This characteristic is visible from many parts of [[Itoshima City]].

The summit features two structures: the upper castle (honmaru) and the lower castle (ninomaru). The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Itoshima City]] and [[Fukuoka City]] ([[Nishi Ward]]) | mountain_range = | type = | first_ascent = | map = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan|coordinates={{Coord|33.548313|130.268646}}|caption=|width=300|float=center|relief=1}}Location of Mount Takasu{{日本の位置情報|33|32|54|130|16|07|Mount Takasu|33.548313,130.268646|Mount Takasu|nocoord=yes}} | osm_zoom = 10 }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), sanchou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Summit of Mount Takasu}}]] '''Mount Takasu''' (高祖山, Takasu-yama) is a mountain with an elevation of 416 meters located on the border of [[Fukuoka City]] and [[Itoshima City]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The mountain is home to the remains of the ancient {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城}} and the medieval {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城}} (also known as Harada Castle). == Name == The origin of the name "Mount Takasu" is unclear. Historical records refer to it as "Takasu," "Peak of Takasu," and "Mount Takasu." Given the presence of {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}} at the western foot of the mountain, it is uncertain whether the shrine derived its name from the mountain or vice versa. The name "Takasu" appears to have been widely recognized in the region.
The mountain is also locally known as "Sleeping Ox Mountain" (臥牛山, Gagyūzan) due to its resemblance to a reclining ox, particularly when viewed from {{ill|Shito Shrine|ja|志登神社}}. This characteristic is visible from many parts of [[Itoshima City]].
South of Mount Takasu lies another peak historically referred to as "Kushifuru Mountain," according to records that note, "It was mistakenly called Kushifuru Mountain due to its location behind a house."‘Itoshima Gunshi’ Fukuoka Prefecture Historical Collections (1927). == Ito Castle == {{main|Ito Castle}} The western slopes of the mountain hosted {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城}}, constructed by {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} (later replaced by {{ill|Saeki no Imami|ja|佐伯今毛人}}) over 12 years, from June of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 8 (756 CE) to February of {{ill|Jingō-keiun|ja|神護景雲}} 2 (768 CE). == Takasu Castle == {{main|Takasu Castle}} While the exact date of Ito Castle's abandonment is unknown, its ruins were later repurposed. In {{ill|Kenchō|ja|建長}} 1 (1249 CE), {{ill|Harada Tanetsugu|ja|原田種継}} and his son {{ill|Harada Taneyori|ja|原田種頼}} built {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城}} on this site. The summit features two structures: the upper castle (honmaru) and the lower castle (ninomaru). The slopes house {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}}, revered by the Harada clan, and {{ill|Kinryūji Temple|ja|金龍寺 (福岡市)}}, the family temple of the Harada.‘Kaisei Harada-ki’ by Kodama Takku (1817). == Hiking == Mount Takasu offers several hiking routes. The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of [[Hakata Bay]], [[Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|ja|雷山}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Cave Tombvi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{citation needed|たかすやま}} [[Category:Mountain-related articles]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima City]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[lld:Mont Takasu]] [[ja:高祖山]] [[Category:pages of u]][[Category:ality]] [[Category:pages of u]][[Category:ality]] [[Category:remove new pages from these]][[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mount Takasu]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mount Takasu]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mount Takasu}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

In {{ill|Kenchō|ja|建長}} 1 (1249 CE), {{ill|Harada Tanetsugu|ja|原田種継}} and his son {{ill|Harada Taneyori|ja|原田種頼}} built {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城}} on this site. The summit features two structures: the upper castle (honmaru) and the lower castle (ninomaru). The slopes house {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}}, revered by the Harada clan, and {{ill|Kinryūji Temple|ja|金龍寺 (福岡市)}}, the family temple of the Harada.‘Kaisei Harada-ki’ by Kodama Takku (1817). == Hiking == Mount Takasu offers several hiking routes. The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of [[Hakata Bay]], [[Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|ja|雷山}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Cave Tombvi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{citation needed|たかすやま}} [[Category:Mountain-related articles]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima City]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:kk]] [[lld:Mont Takasu]] [[ja:高祖山]] [[Category:ality]] [[Category:ality]] [[Category:remove new pages from these]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mount Takasu]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mount Takasu]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mount Takasu}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

Given the presence of {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}} at the western foot of the mountain, it is uncertain whether the shrine derived its name from the mountain or vice versa. The name "Takasu" appears to have been widely recognized in the region.
The mountain is also locally known as "Sleeping Ox Mountain" (臥牛山, Gagyūzan) due to its resemblance to a reclining ox, particularly when viewed from {{ill|Shito Shrine|ja|志登神社}}. This characteristic is visible from many parts of [[Itoshima City]].
South of Mount Takasu lies another peak historically referred to as "Kushifuru Mountain," according to records that note, "It was mistakenly called Kushifuru Mountain due to its location behind a house."‘Itoshima Gunshi’ Fukuoka Prefecture Historical Collections (1927). == Ito Castle == {{main|Ito Castle}} The western slopes of the mountain hosted {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城}}, constructed by {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} (later replaced by {{ill|Saeki no Imami|ja|佐伯今毛人}}) over 12 years, from June of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 8 (756 CE) to February of {{ill|Jingō-keiun|ja|神護景雲}} 2 (768 CE). == Takasu Castle == {{main|Takasu Castle}} While the exact date of Ito Castle's abandonment is unknown, its ruins were later repurposed. In {{ill|Kenchō|ja|建長}} 1 (1249 CE), {{ill|Harada Tanetsugu|ja|原田種継}} and his son {{ill|Harada Taneyori|ja|原田種頼}} built {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城}} on this site. The summit features two structures: the upper castle (honmaru) and the lower castle (ninomaru). The slopes house {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}}, revered by the Harada clan, and {{ill|Kinryūji Temple|ja|金龍寺 (福岡市)}}, the family temple of the Harada.‘Kaisei Harada-ki’ by Kodama Takku (1817). == Hiking == Mount Takasu offers several hiking routes. The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of [[Hakata Bay]], [[Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|ja|雷山}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Cave Tombvi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{citation needed|たかすやま}} [[Category:Mountain-related articles]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima City]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[Category:Takasu Mountain]][[lld:Mont Takasu]] [[ja:高祖山]] [[Category:ality]] [[Category:ality]] [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mount Takasu]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mount Takasu]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mount Takasu}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mount Takasu]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Takasu | photo = [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), enkei.jpg|300px|Mount Takasu]] | photo_caption = Distant view of Mount Takasu | elevation_m = 416 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|54|N|130|16|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
[[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Itoshima City]] and [[Fukuoka City]] ([[Nishi Ward]]) | mountain_range = | type = | first_ascent = | map = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan|coordinates={{Coord|33.548313|130.268646}}|caption=|width=300|float=center|relief=1}}Location of Mount Takasu{{日本の位置情報|33|32|54|130|16|07|Mount Takasu|33.548313,130.268646|Mount Takasu|nocoord=yes}} | osm_zoom = 10 }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), sanchou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Summit of Mount Takasu}}]] '''Mount Takasu''' (高祖山, Takasu-yama) is a mountain with an elevation of 416 meters located on the border of [[Fukuoka City]] and [[Itoshima City]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The mountain is home to the remains of the ancient {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城|12=draft}} and the medieval {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城|12=draft}} (also known as Harada Castle). == Name == The origin of the name "Mount Takasu" is unclear. Historical records refer to it as "Takasu," "Peak of Takasu," and "Mount Takasu." Given the presence of {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社|12=draft}} at the western foot of the mountain, it is uncertain whether the shrine derived its name from the mountain or vice versa. The name "Takasu" appears to have been widely recognized in the region.
The mountain is also locally known as "Sleeping Ox Mountain" (臥牛山, Gagyūzan) due to its resemblance to a reclining ox, particularly when viewed from {{ill|Shito Shrine|ja|志登神社|12=draft}}. This characteristic is visible from many parts of [[Itoshima City]].
South of Mount Takasu lies another peak historically referred to as "Kushifuru Mountain," according to records that note, "It was mistakenly called Kushifuru Mountain due to its location behind a house."‘Itoshima Gunshi’ Fukuoka Prefecture Historical Collections (1927). == Ito Castle == {{main|Ito Castle}} The western slopes of the mountain hosted {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城|12=draft}}, constructed by {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft}} (later replaced by {{ill|Saeki no Imami|ja|佐伯今毛人|12=draft}}) over 12 years, from June of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝|12=draft}} 8 (756 CE) to February of {{ill|Jingō-keiun|ja|神護景雲|12=draft}} 2 (768 CE). == Takasu Castle == {{main|Takasu Castle}} While the exact date of Ito Castle's abandonment is unknown, its ruins were later repurposed. In {{ill|Kenchō|ja|建長|12=draft}} 1 (1249 CE), {{ill|Harada Tanetsugu|ja|原田種継|12=draft}} and his son {{ill|Harada Taneyori|ja|原田種頼|12=draft}} built {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城|12=draft}} on this site. The summit features two structures: the upper castle (honmaru) and the lower castle (ninomaru). The slopes house {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社|12=draft}}, revered by the Harada clan, and {{ill|Kinryūji Temple|ja|金龍寺 (福岡市)|12=draft}}, the family temple of the Harada.‘Kaisei Harada-ki’ by Kodama Takku (1817). == Hiking == Mount Takasu offers several hiking routes. The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター|12=draft}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳|12=draft}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of [[Hakata Bay]], [[Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|ja|雷山|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地|12=draft}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Cave Tombvi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{citation needed|たかすやま}} [[lld:Mont Takasu]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountain-related articles]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima City]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Takasu Mountain]] [[Category:ality]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:高祖山]] [[sn:Mount Takasu]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mount Takasu}}

Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of [[Hakata Bay]], [[Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|ja|雷山|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地|12=draft}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Cave Tombvi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{citation needed|たかすやま}} [[lld:Mont Takasu]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountain-related articles]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima City]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Takasu Mountain]] [[Category:ality]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:高祖山]] [[sn:Mount Takasu]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mount Takasu}}

The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター|12=draft}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳|12=draft}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of [[Hakata Bay]], [[Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|ja|雷山|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地|12=draft}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Cave Tombvi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{citation needed|たかすやま}} [[lld:Mont Takasu]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountain-related articles]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima City]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Takasu Mountain]] [[Category:ality]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:高祖山]] [[sn:Mount Takasu]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mount Takasu}}

{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Takasu | photo = [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), enkei.jpg|300px|Mount Takasu]] | photo_caption = Distant view of Mount Takasu | elevation_m = 416 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|54|N|130|16|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
[[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Itoshima City]] and [[Fukuoka City]] ([[Nishi Ward]]) | mountain_range = | type = | first_ascent = | map = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan|coordinates={{Coord|33.548313|130.268646}}|caption=|width=300|float=center|relief=1}}Location of Mount Takasu{{日本の位置情報|33|32|54|130|16|07|Mount Takasu|33.548313,130.268646|Mount Takasu|nocoord=yes}} | osm_zoom = 10 }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), sanchou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Summit of Mount Takasu}}]] '''Mount Takasu''' (高祖山, Takasu-yama) is a mountain with an elevation of 416 meters located on the border of [[Fukuoka City]] and [[Itoshima City]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The mountain is home to the remains of the ancient {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城|12=draft}} and the medieval {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城|12=draft}} (also known as Harada Castle). == Name == The origin of the name "Mount Takasu" is unclear. Historical records refer to it as "Takasu," "Peak of Takasu," and "Mount Takasu." Given the presence of {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社|12=draft}} at the western foot of the mountain, it is uncertain whether the shrine derived its name from the mountain or vice versa. The name "Takasu" appears to have been widely recognized in the region.
The mountain is also locally known as "Sleeping Ox Mountain" (臥牛山, Gagyūzan) due to its resemblance to a reclining ox, particularly when viewed from {{ill|Shito Shrine|ja|志登神社|12=draft}}. This characteristic is visible from many parts of [[Itoshima City]].
South of Mount Takasu lies another peak historically referred to as "Kushifuru Mountain," according to records that note, "It was mistakenly called Kushifuru Mountain due to its location behind a house."‘Itoshima Gunshi’ Fukuoka Prefecture Historical Collections (1927). == Ito Castle == {{main|Ito Castle}} The western slopes of the mountain hosted {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城|12=draft}}, constructed by {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft}} (later replaced by {{ill|Saeki no Imami|ja|佐伯今毛人|12=draft}}) over 12 years, from June of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝|12=draft}} 8 (756 CE) to February of {{ill|Jingō-keiun|ja|神護景雲|12=draft}} 2 (768 CE). == Takasu Castle == {{main|Takasu Castle}} While the exact date of Ito Castle's abandonment is unknown, its ruins were later repurposed. In {{ill|Kenchō|ja|建長|12=draft}} 1 (1249 CE), {{ill|Harada Tanetsugu|ja|原田種継|12=draft}} and his son {{ill|Harada Taneyori|ja|原田種頼|12=draft}} built {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城|12=draft}} on this site. The summit features two structures: the upper castle (honmaru) and the lower castle (ninomaru). The slopes house {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社|12=draft}}, revered by the Harada clan, and {{ill|Kinryūji Temple|ja|金龍寺 (福岡市)|12=draft}}, the family temple of the Harada.‘Kaisei Harada-ki’ by Kodama Takku (1817). == Hiking == Mount Takasu offers several hiking routes. The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター|12=draft}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳|12=draft}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of [[Hakata Bay]], [[Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|ja|雷山|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地|12=draft}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Cave Tombvi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{citation needed|たかすやま}} [[lld:Mont Takasu]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountain-related articles]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima City]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Takasu Mountain]] [[Category:ality]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:高祖山]] [[sn:Mount Takasu]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mount Takasu}}

revision.comment: 31 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:高祖山 up to rev 99377322
revision.text:
{{Infobox 山 |名称 = 高祖山 |画像 = [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), enkei.jpg|300px|高祖山]] |画像キャプション = 遠景 |標高 = 416 |座標 = {{Coord|33|32|54|N|130|16|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} |所在地 = {{JPN}}
[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]・[[福岡市]][[西区 (福岡市)|西区]] |山系= |種類 = |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan|coordinates={{Coord|33.548313|130.268646}}|caption=|width=300|float=center|relief=1}}高祖山の位置{{日本の位置情報|33|32|54|130|16|07|高祖山|33.548313,130.268646|高祖山|nocoord=yes}} |OSMズーム = 10 }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), sanchou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|山頂}}]] '''高祖山'''（たかすやま）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]]と[[糸島市]]の境に位置する標高416mの山である。古代の[[怡土城]]跡と中世の[[高祖城]]跡（原田城跡）がある。 == 名称 == 「高祖山」のその名の由来は正確には不明で、古い文献には「高祖」「高祖の峰」「高祖山」とある。西の麓に[[高祖神社]]が存在する事から、この神社が山の名の由来、あるいは、先に山の「高祖」から「高祖神社」となったのか、はっきりとは決め難いが、「高祖」の名称はこの一帯にあったらしい。
また、その山容が、牛が長々と寝そべっている姿のように見える（特に[[志登神社]]から見るその山容は牛が寝そべっている姿に見える事）から'''臥牛山'''と地元では言われ、[[糸島市]]内のかなりの地域からその山容を望むことができる。
高祖山には南にもう一つの峰があり、これを「'''くしふる山'''」と呼んでいた、と推定される（文献には、「椚(クヌギ)～、民家の後ろにあるをクシフル山と云ひしを誤りて椚と云ふに至れり」とある）『糸島郡誌』福岡県郷土史叢刊（[[1927年]]）。 == 怡土城 == {{main|怡土城}} 西斜面一帯には、[[天平勝宝]]8歳6月（[[756年]]）から[[神護景雲]]2年2月（[[768年]]）まで約12年の歳月をかけて、[[吉備真備]]（途中から[[佐伯今毛人]]に交代）が築いた[[怡土城]]があった。 == 高祖城 == {{main|高祖城}} [[怡土城]]が廃城になった時期は不明だが、この遺構を利用して、[[建長]]元年（[[1249年]]）、[[原田種継]]・[[原田種頼|種頼]]父子が[[高祖城]]を築城した。山頂には上ノ城（本丸）と下ノ城（二ノ丸）がある。山腹には[[高祖城]]主[[原田氏]]の崇敬が厚かった[[高祖神社]]と原田家菩提寺の[[金龍寺 (福岡市)|金龍寺]]がある『改正原田記』[[児玉琢]]/著（[[1817年]]）。 == 登山 == 今宿野外活動センターを拠点に、南の高地山、東の[[叶岳]]とミニ縦走を楽しむことができる。その他高来寺や如意、[[高祖神社]]裏手からの登山道がある。遊歩道はよく整備されており、1〜2時間ほどで山頂に到達できる。山頂は木が茂り眺望があまり良くないが、途中の尾根から東に[[博多湾]]や[[福岡市]]街、西に[[可也山]]や[[浮嶽]]、[[羽金山]]、[[雷山]]など[[脊振山]]系の山々が見られる。 == 出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ 高祖山 - 福岡市の公式シティガイド よかなび] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 高祖山登山歩道 - 福岡県観光情報 クロスロードふくおか] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html 怡土城跡（いとじょうあと） - 糸島市ホームページ] {{デフォルトソート:たかすやま}} [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:福岡県の山]] [[Category:福岡市西区の地理]] [[Category:糸島市の地理]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]]

[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]・[[福岡市]][[西区 (福岡市)|西区]] |山系= |種類 = |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan|coordinates={{Coord|33.548313|130.268646}}|caption=|width=300|float=center|relief=1}}高祖山の位置{{日本の位置情報|33|32|54|130|16|07|高祖山|33.548313,130.268646|高祖山|nocoord=yes}} |OSMズーム = 10 }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), sanchou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|山頂}}]] '''高祖山'''（たかすやま）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]]と[[糸島市]]の境に位置する標高416mの山である。古代の[[怡土城]]跡と中世の[[高祖城]]跡（原田城跡）がある。 == 名称 == 「高祖山」のその名の由来は正確には不明で、古い文献には「高祖」「高祖の峰」「高祖山」とある。西の麓に[[高祖神社]]が存在する事から、この神社が山の名の由来、あるいは、先に山の「高祖」から「高祖神社」となったのか、はっきりとは決め難いが、「高祖」の名称はこの一帯にあったらしい。
また、その山容が、牛が長々と寝そべっている姿のように見える（特に[[志登神社]]から見るその山容は牛が寝そべっている姿に見える事）から'''臥牛山'''と地元では言われ、[[糸島市]]内のかなりの地域からその山容を望むことができる。
高祖山には南にもう一つの峰があり、これを「'''くしふる山'''」と呼んでいた、と推定される（文献には、「椚(クヌギ)～、民家の後ろにあるをクシフル山と云ひしを誤りて椚と云ふに至れり」とある）『糸島郡誌』福岡県郷土史叢刊（[[1927年]]）。 == 怡土城 == {{main|怡土城}} 西斜面一帯には、[[天平勝宝]]8歳6月（[[756年]]）から[[神護景雲]]2年2月（[[768年]]）まで約12年の歳月をかけて、[[吉備真備]]（途中から[[佐伯今毛人]]に交代）が築いた[[怡土城]]があった。 == 高祖城 == {{main|高祖城}} [[怡土城]]が廃城になった時期は不明だが、この遺構を利用して、[[建長]]元年（[[1249年]]）、[[原田種継]]・[[原田種頼|種頼]]父子が[[高祖城]]を築城した。山頂には上ノ城（本丸）と下ノ城（二ノ丸）がある。山腹には[[高祖城]]主[[原田氏]]の崇敬が厚かった[[高祖神社]]と原田家菩提寺の[[金龍寺 (福岡市)|金龍寺]]がある『改正原田記』[[児玉琢]]/著（[[1817年]]）。 == 登山 == 今宿野外活動センターを拠点に、南の高地山、東の[[叶岳]]とミニ縦走を楽しむことができる。その他高来寺や如意、[[高祖神社]]裏手からの登山道がある。遊歩道はよく整備されており、1〜2時間ほどで山頂に到達できる。山頂は木が茂り眺望があまり良くないが、途中の尾根から東に[[博多湾]]や[[福岡市]]街、西に[[可也山]]や[[浮嶽]]、[[羽金山]]、[[雷山]]など[[脊振山]]系の山々が見られる。 == 出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ 高祖山 - 福岡市の公式シティガイド よかなび] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 高祖山登山歩道 - 福岡県観光情報 クロスロードふくおか] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html 怡土城跡（いとじょうあと） - 糸島市ホームページ] {{デフォルトソート:たかすやま}} [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:福岡県の山]] [[Category:福岡市西区の地理]] [[Category:糸島市の地理]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]]

{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Takasu | photo = [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), enkei.jpg|300px|Mount Takasu]] | photo_caption = Distant view of Mount Takasu | elevation_m = 416 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|54|N|130|16|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
[[Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[Itoshima City]] and [[Fukuoka City]] ([[Nishi Ward]]) | mountain_range = | type = | first_ascent = | map = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan|coordinates={{Coord|33.548313|130.268646}}|caption=|width=300|float=center|relief=1}}Location of Mount Takasu{{日本の位置情報|33|32|54|130|16|07|Mount Takasu|33.548313,130.268646|Mount Takasu|nocoord=yes}} | osm_zoom = 10 }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), sanchou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Summit of Mount Takasu}}]] '''Mount Takasu''' (高祖山, Takasu-yama) is a mountain with an elevation of 416 meters located on the border of [[Fukuoka City]] and [[Itoshima City]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The mountain is home to the remains of the ancient {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城}} and the medieval {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城}} (also known as Harada Castle). == Name == The origin of the name "Mount Takasu" is unclear. Historical records refer to it as "Takasu," "Peak of Takasu," and "Mount Takasu." Given the presence of {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}} at the western foot of the mountain, it is uncertain whether the shrine derived its name from the mountain or vice versa. The name "Takasu" appears to have been widely recognized in the region.
The mountain is also locally known as "Sleeping Ox Mountain" (臥牛山, Gagyūzan) due to its resemblance to a reclining ox, particularly when viewed from {{ill|Shito Shrine|ja|志登神社}}. This characteristic is visible from many parts of [[Itoshima City]].
South of Mount Takasu lies another peak historically referred to as "Kushifuru Mountain," according to records that note, "It was mistakenly called Kushifuru Mountain due to its location behind a house."‘Itoshima Gunshi’ Fukuoka Prefecture Historical Collections (1927). == Ito Castle == {{main|Ito Castle}} The western slopes of the mountain hosted {{ill|Ito Castle|ja|怡土城}}, constructed by {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|ja|吉備真備}} (later replaced by {{ill|Saeki no Imami|ja|佐伯今毛人}}) over 12 years, from June of {{ill|Tenpyō Shōhō|ja|天平勝宝}} 8 (756 CE) to February of {{ill|Jingō-keiun|ja|神護景雲}} 2 (768 CE). == Takasu Castle == {{main|Takasu Castle}} While the exact date of Ito Castle's abandonment is unknown, its ruins were later repurposed. In {{ill|Kenchō|ja|建長}} 1 (1249 CE), {{ill|Harada Tanetsugu|ja|原田種継}} and his son {{ill|Harada Taneyori|ja|原田種頼}} built {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城}} on this site. The summit features two structures: the upper castle (honmaru) and the lower castle (ninomaru). The slopes house {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社}}, revered by the Harada clan, and {{ill|Kinryūji Temple|ja|金龍寺 (福岡市)}}, the family temple of the Harada.‘Kaisei Harada-ki’ by Kodama Takku (1817). == Hiking == Mount Takasu offers several hiking routes. The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of [[Hakata Bay]], [[Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|ja|雷山}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Cave Tombvi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{citation needed|たかすやま}} [[lld:Mont Takasu]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountain-related articles]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima City]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Takasu Mountain]] [[Category:ality]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:高祖山]] [[sn:Mount Takasu]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mount Takasu}} {{translated page|ja|高祖山|version=99377322|comment=Imported full ja history}}
