Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of {{ill|Hakata Bay|en|Hakata Bay|WD=Q2344806|lt=Hakata Bay}}, [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kaya|1=Mount Kaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411807}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Uki|1=Mount Uki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558529}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hagan|1=Hagane Yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610393}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|en|Mount Rai|WD=Q11660169|lt=Mount Rai}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sefuri Mountains|1=Sefuri Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612199}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Yokoanabovi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{translated page|ja|高祖山|version=99377322|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[lld:Mont Takasu]] [[ja:高祖山]] [[ceb:Takasu Yama (bukid sa Hapon, Fukuoka-ken)]] {{Commons category|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} [[zh:高祖山]] {{wikidata link|Q18337213}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima]] [[Category:Geography of Nishi-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.comment: Bot: fix ill template destination links
revision.text:
[[ceb:Takasu Yama (bukid sa Hapon, Fukuoka-ken)]][[co:commons:Category:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)]][[ja:高祖山]][[lld:Mont Takasu]][[zh:高祖山]] [[ceb:Takasu Yama (bukid sa Hapon, Fukuoka-ken)]][[co:commons:Category:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)]][[ja:高祖山]][[lld:Mont Takasu]][[zh:高祖山]] [[ceb:Takasu Yama (bukid sa Hapon, Fukuoka-ken)]][[sn:commons:Category:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)]][[ja:高祖山]][[lld:Mont Takasu]][[zh:高祖山]] {{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Takasu | photo = [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), enkei.jpg|300px|Mount Takasu]] | photo_caption = Distant view of Mount Takasu | elevation_m = 416 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|54|N|130|16|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, [[Itoshima City]] and [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]] ([[Nishi Ward]]) | mountain_range = | type = | first_ascent = | map = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan|coordinates={{Coord|33.548313|130.268646}}|caption=|width=300|float=center|relief=1}}Location of Mount Takasu{{日本の位置情報|33|32|54|130|16|07|Mount Takasu|33.548313,130.268646|Mount Takasu|nocoord=yes}} | osm_zoom = 10 }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), sanchou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Summit of Mount Takasu}}]] '''Mount Takasu''' (高祖山, Takasu-yama) is a mountain with an elevation of 416 meters located on the border of [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]] and [[Itoshima City]] in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. The mountain is home to the remains of the ancient {{ill|Ito Castle|en|Ito Castle|WD=Q11491550|lt=Ito Castle|1=Ito Castle}} and the medieval {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Castle|1=Takasu Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672658}} (also known as Harada Castle). == Name == The origin of the name "Mount Takasu" is unclear. Historical records refer to it as "Takasu," "Peak of Takasu," and "Mount Takasu." Given the presence of {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Shrine|qq=}} at the western foot of the mountain, it is uncertain whether the shrine derived its name from the mountain or vice versa. The name "Takasu" appears to have been widely recognized in the region.
The mountain is also locally known as "Sleeping Ox Mountain" (臥牛山, Gagyūzan) due to its resemblance to a reclining ox, particularly when viewed from {{ill|Shito Shrine|ja|志登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shito Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This characteristic is visible from many parts of [[Itoshima City]].
South of Mount Takasu lies another peak historically referred to as "Kushifuru Mountain," according to records that note, "It was mistakenly called Kushifuru Mountain due to its location behind a house."‘Itoshima Gunshi’ Fukuoka Prefecture Historical Collections (1927). == Ito Castle == {{main|Ito Castle}} The western slopes of the mountain hosted {{ill|Ito Castle|en|Ito Castle|WD=Q11491550|lt=Ito Castle|1=Ito Castle}}, constructed by {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}} (later replaced by {{ill|Saeki no Imami|ja|佐伯今毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Imami|1=Saeki no Imaemishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382787}}) over 12 years, from June of {{ill|Tenpyō-shōhō|en|Tenpyō-shōhō|WD=Q1373169|lt=Tenpyō Shōhō|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 8 (756 CE) to February of {{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|WD=Q1373049|lt=Jingō-keiun|1=Jingo-keiun}} 2 (768 CE). == Takasu Castle == {{main|Takasu Castle}} While the exact date of Ito Castle's abandonment is unknown, its ruins were later repurposed. In {{ill|Kenchō|en|Kenchō|WD=Q1194270|lt=Kenchō|1=Kenchō}} 1 (1249 CE), {{ill|Harada Tanetsugu|ja|原田種継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Tanetsugu|1=Harada Tanetsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18234614}} and his son {{ill|Harada Taneyori|ja|原田種頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Taneyori|1=Q17221517|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221517}} built {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Castle|1=Takasu Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672658}} on this site. The summit features two structures: the upper castle (honmaru) and the lower castle (ninomaru). The slopes house {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Shrine|qq=}}, revered by the Harada clan, and {{ill|Kinryūji Temple|ja|金龍寺 (福岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinryūji Temple|1=Kinryu-ji Temple (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648035}}, the family temple of the Harada.‘Kaisei Harada-ki’ by Kodama Takku (1817). == Hiking == Mount Takasu offers several hiking routes. The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanōdake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of {{ill|Hakata Bay|en|Hakata Bay|WD=Q2344806|lt=Hakata Bay|1=Hakata Bay}}, [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kaya|1=Q11411807|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411807}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Uki|1=Mount Uki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558529}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hagan|1=Hagane Yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610393}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|en|Mount Rai|WD=Q11660169|lt=Mount Rai|1=Mount Rai}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sefuri Mountains|1=Sefuri Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612199}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Yokoanabovi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{translated page|ja|高祖山|version=99377322|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[lld:Mont Takasu]] [[ja:高祖山]] [[ceb:Takasu Yama (bukid sa Hapon, Fukuoka-ken)]] {{Commons category|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} [[zh:高祖山]] {{wikidata link|Q18337213}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Geography of Itoshima]] [[Category:Geography of Nishi-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanōdake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of {{ill|Hakata Bay|en|Hakata Bay|WD=Q2344806|lt=Hakata Bay|1=Hakata Bay}}, [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kaya|1=Q11411807|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411807}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Uki|1=Mount Uki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558529}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hagan|1=Hagane Yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610393}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|en|Mount Rai|WD=Q11660169|lt=Mount Rai|1=Mount Rai}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sefuri Mountains|1=Sefuri Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612199}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Yokoanabovi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{translated page|ja|高祖山|version=99377322|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[lld:Mont Takasu]] [[ja:高祖山]] [[ceb:Takasu Yama (bukid sa Hapon, Fukuoka-ken)]] {{Commons category|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} [[zh:高祖山]] {{wikidata link|Q18337213}} [[Category:Geography of Itoshima]] [[Category:Geography of Nishi-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ceb:Takasu Yama (bukid sa Hapon, Fukuoka-ken)]][[co:commons:Category:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)]][[ja:高祖山]][[lld:Mont Takasu]][[zh:高祖山]] [[ceb:Takasu Yama (bukid sa Hapon, Fukuoka-ken)]][[co:commons:Category:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)]][[ja:高祖山]][[lld:Mont Takasu]][[zh:高祖山]] [[ceb:Takasu Yama (bukid sa Hapon, Fukuoka-ken)]][[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)]][[ja:高祖山]][[lld:Mont Takasu]][[zh:高祖山]] {{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Takasu | photo = [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), enkei.jpg|300px|Mount Takasu]] | photo_caption = Distant view of Mount Takasu | elevation_m = 416 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|54|N|130|16|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, [[Itoshima City]] and [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]] ([[Nishi Ward]]) | mountain_range = | type = | first_ascent = | map = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan|coordinates={{Coord|33.548313|130.268646}}|caption=|width=300|float=center|relief=1}}Location of Mount Takasu{{日本の位置情報|33|32|54|130|16|07|Mount Takasu|33.548313,130.268646|Mount Takasu|nocoord=yes}} | osm_zoom = 10 }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), sanchou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Summit of Mount Takasu}}]] '''Mount Takasu''' (高祖山, Takasu-yama) is a mountain with an elevation of 416 meters located on the border of [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]] and [[Itoshima City]] in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. The mountain is home to the remains of the ancient {{ill|Ito Castle|en|Ito Castle|WD=Q11491550|lt=Ito Castle|1=Ito Castle}} and the medieval {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Castle|1=Takasu Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672658}} (also known as Harada Castle). == Name == The origin of the name "Mount Takasu" is unclear. Historical records refer to it as "Takasu," "Peak of Takasu," and "Mount Takasu." Given the presence of {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Shrine|qq=}} at the western foot of the mountain, it is uncertain whether the shrine derived its name from the mountain or vice versa. The name "Takasu" appears to have been widely recognized in the region.
The mountain is also locally known as "Sleeping Ox Mountain" (臥牛山, Gagyūzan) due to its resemblance to a reclining ox, particularly when viewed from {{ill|Shito Shrine|ja|志登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shito Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This characteristic is visible from many parts of [[Itoshima City]].
South of Mount Takasu lies another peak historically referred to as "Kushifuru Mountain," according to records that note, "It was mistakenly called Kushifuru Mountain due to its location behind a house."‘Itoshima Gunshi’ Fukuoka Prefecture Historical Collections (1927). == Ito Castle == {{main|Ito Castle}} The western slopes of the mountain hosted {{ill|Ito Castle|en|Ito Castle|WD=Q11491550|lt=Ito Castle|1=Ito Castle}}, constructed by {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|WD=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}} (later replaced by {{ill|Saeki no Imami|ja|佐伯今毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Imami|1=Saeki no Imaemishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382787}}) over 12 years, from June of {{ill|Tenpyō-shōhō|en|Tenpyō-shōhō|WD=Q1373169|lt=Tenpyō Shōhō|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 8 (756 CE) to February of {{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|WD=Q1373049|lt=Jingō-keiun|1=Jingo-keiun}} 2 (768 CE). == Takasu Castle == {{main|Takasu Castle}} While the exact date of Ito Castle's abandonment is unknown, its ruins were later repurposed. In {{ill|Kenchō|en|Kenchō|WD=Q1194270|lt=Kenchō|1=Kenchō}} 1 (1249 CE), {{ill|Harada Tanetsugu|ja|原田種継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Tanetsugu|1=Harada Tanetsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18234614}} and his son {{ill|Harada Taneyori|ja|原田種頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Taneyori|1=Q17221517|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221517}} built {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Castle|1=Takasu Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672658}} on this site. The summit features two structures: the upper castle (honmaru) and the lower castle (ninomaru). The slopes house {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Shrine|qq=}}, revered by the Harada clan, and {{ill|Kinryūji Temple|ja|金龍寺 (福岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinryūji Temple|1=Kinryu-ji Temple (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11648035}}, the family temple of the Harada.‘Kaisei Harada-ki’ by Kodama Takku (1817). == Hiking == Mount Takasu offers several hiking routes. The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanōdake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of {{ill|Hakata Bay|en|Hakata Bay|WD=Q2344806|lt=Hakata Bay|1=Hakata Bay}}, [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kaya|1=Q11411807|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411807}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Uki|1=Mount Uki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558529}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hagan|1=Hagane Yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11610393}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|en|Mount Rai|WD=Q11660169|lt=Mount Rai|1=Mount Rai}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sefuri Mountains|1=Sefuri Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612199}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Yokoanabovi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{translated page|ja|高祖山|version=99377322|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[lld:Mont Takasu]] [[ja:高祖山]] [[ceb:Takasu Yama (bukid sa Hapon, Fukuoka-ken)]] {{Commons category|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} [[zh:高祖山]] {{wikidata link|Q18337213}} [[Category:Geography of Itoshima]] [[Category:Geography of Nishi-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanōdake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of {{ill|Hakata Bay|en|Hakata Bay|qid=Q2344806|lt=Hakata Bay|1=Hakata Bay}}, [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kaya|1=Q11411807|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11411807}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Uki|1=Mount Uki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11558529}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hagan|1=Hagane Yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11610393}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|en|Mount Rai|qid=Q11660169|lt=Mount Rai|1=Mount Rai}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sefuri Mountains|1=Sefuri Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11612199}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Yokoanabovi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{translated page|ja|高祖山|version=99377322|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[lld:Mont Takasu]] [[ja:高祖山]] [[ceb:Takasu Yama (bukid sa Hapon, Fukuoka-ken)]] {{Commons category|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} [[zh:高祖山]] {{wikidata link|Q18337213}} [[Category:Geography of Itoshima]] [[Category:Geography of Nishi-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Takasu | photo = [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), enkei.jpg|300px|Mount Takasu]] | photo_caption = Distant view of Mount Takasu | elevation_m = 416 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|54|N|130|16|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, [[Itoshima City]] and [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]] ([[Nishi Ward]]) | mountain_range = | type = | first_ascent = | map = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan|coordinates={{Coord|33.548313|130.268646}}|caption=|width=300|float=center|relief=1}}Location of Mount Takasu{{日本の位置情報|33|32|54|130|16|07|Mount Takasu|33.548313,130.268646|Mount Takasu|nocoord=yes}} | osm_zoom = 10 }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), sanchou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Summit of Mount Takasu}}]] '''Mount Takasu''' (高祖山, Takasu-yama) is a mountain with an elevation of 416 meters located on the border of [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]] and [[Itoshima City]] in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. The mountain is home to the remains of the ancient {{ill|Ito Castle|en|Ito Castle|qid=Q11491550|lt=Ito Castle|1=Ito Castle}} and the medieval {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Castle|1=Takasu Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11672658}} (also known as Harada Castle). == Name == The origin of the name "Mount Takasu" is unclear. Historical records refer to it as "Takasu," "Peak of Takasu," and "Mount Takasu." Given the presence of {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Shrine|qq=}} at the western foot of the mountain, it is uncertain whether the shrine derived its name from the mountain or vice versa. The name "Takasu" appears to have been widely recognized in the region.
The mountain is also locally known as "Sleeping Ox Mountain" (臥牛山, Gagyūzan) due to its resemblance to a reclining ox, particularly when viewed from {{ill|Shito Shrine|ja|志登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shito Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This characteristic is visible from many parts of [[Itoshima City]].
South of Mount Takasu lies another peak historically referred to as "Kushifuru Mountain," according to records that note, "It was mistakenly called Kushifuru Mountain due to its location behind a house."‘Itoshima Gunshi’ Fukuoka Prefecture Historical Collections (1927). == Ito Castle == {{main|Ito Castle}} The western slopes of the mountain hosted {{ill|Ito Castle|en|Ito Castle|qid=Q11491550|lt=Ito Castle|1=Ito Castle}}, constructed by {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|qid=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}} (later replaced by {{ill|Saeki no Imami|ja|佐伯今毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Imami|1=Saeki no Imaemishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11382787}}) over 12 years, from June of {{ill|Tenpyō-shōhō|en|Tenpyō-shōhō|qid=Q1373169|lt=Tenpyō Shōhō|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 8 (756 CE) to February of {{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|qid=Q1373049|lt=Jingō-keiun|1=Jingo-keiun}} 2 (768 CE). == Takasu Castle == {{main|Takasu Castle}} While the exact date of Ito Castle's abandonment is unknown, its ruins were later repurposed. In {{ill|Kenchō|en|Kenchō|qid=Q1194270|lt=Kenchō|1=Kenchō}} 1 (1249 CE), {{ill|Harada Tanetsugu|ja|原田種継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Tanetsugu|1=Harada Tanetsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18234614}} and his son {{ill|Harada Taneyori|ja|原田種頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Taneyori|1=Q17221517|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17221517}} built {{ill|Takasu Castle|ja|高祖城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Castle|1=Takasu Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11672658}} on this site. The summit features two structures: the upper castle (honmaru) and the lower castle (ninomaru). The slopes house {{ill|Takasu Shrine|ja|高祖神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Shrine|qq=}}, revered by the Harada clan, and {{ill|Kinryūji Temple|ja|金龍寺 (福岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinryūji Temple|1=Kinryu-ji Temple (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11648035}}, the family temple of the Harada.‘Kaisei Harada-ki’ by Kodama Takku (1817). == Hiking == Mount Takasu offers several hiking routes. The trail from the {{ill|Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja|今宿野外活動センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Outdoor Activity Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} connects to nearby peaks, including Takochi Mountain and {{ill|Kanōdake|ja|叶岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanōdake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, allowing for a mini traversing experience. Other routes include paths from Takakuraji, Nyoi, and the rear of Takasu Shrine. The trails are well-maintained, and the summit can be reached within 1–2 hours. Although the summit itself is wooded and offers limited views, the ridge trails provide expansive vistas of {{ill|Hakata Bay|en|Hakata Bay|qid=Q2344806|lt=Hakata Bay|1=Hakata Bay}}, [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], and the surrounding mountains, such as {{ill|Mount Kaya|ja|可也山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kaya|1=Q11411807|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11411807}}, {{ill|Mount Uki|ja|浮嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Uki|1=Mount Uki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11558529}}, {{ill|Mount Hagan|ja|羽金山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hagan|1=Hagane Yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11610393}}, {{ill|Mount Rai|en|Mount Rai|qid=Q11660169|lt=Mount Rai|1=Mount Rai}}, and the {{ill|Sefuri Mountains|ja|脊振山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sefuri Mountains|1=Sefuri Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11612199}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} * [https://yokanavi.com/spot/26895/ Mount Takasu - Fukuoka City's official guide Yokoanabovi] * [http://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/jp/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000195&isSpot=1 Mount Takasu Trails - Fukuoka Tourism Information] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/itojou-ato.html Ito Castle Ruins - Itoshima City Homepage] {{translated page|ja|高祖山|version=99377322|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q18337213|ceb|Takasu Yama (bukid sa Hapon, Fukuoka-ken)|co|commons:Category:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka)|ja|高祖山|lld|Mont Takasu|zh|高祖山}} [[Category:Geography of Itoshima]] [[Category:Geography of Nishi-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
